title: Alectryon glaber
taxonomy: Alectryon glaber is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. It belongs to the genus Alectryon, which is a group of plants native to Australia and New Guinea. The genus Alectryon is part of the larger family Sapindaceae, which includes trees and shrubs commonly known as soapberries or lychees. The classification of Alectryon glaber reflects its unique characteristics and relationships with other plants within the Sapindaceae family. Further studies on the plant's morphology and genetics have helped to solidify its place within the Sapindaceae family.

intro: Alectryon glaber, also known as the smooth raintree or Chinese parasol tree, is a deciduous tree native to the tropical regions of Asia, characterized by its attractive, umbrella-shaped canopy and smooth bark. The tree has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, wounds, and skin infections.
